# Yuku

> A high-performance JavaScript and TypeScript parser and toolchain, written in Zig. Faster than Oxc, SWC, Babel, and Acorn. 100% ECMAScript spec compliant, zero dependencies.

Yuku is a JavaScript/TypeScript compiler toolchain in Zig. It provides a fast, spec-compliant parser with an ESTree-compatible AST, a traverser system (basic, scoped, semantic, transform), and semantic analysis. It is designed for correctness, performance, and clarity.

## Performance

Yuku is faster than Oxc, SWC, Babel, and Acorn. Native parsing is competitive with Oxc and roughly 2× faster than SWC. On the JavaScript (npm) side, Yuku is 3–5× faster than alternatives, driven by a flat, near-binary AST transfer format.

Live benchmark sources:

- Native: https://github.com/yuku-toolchain/ecmascript-parser-benchmark-native
- JavaScript (npm): https://github.com/yuku-toolchain/ecmascript-parser-benchmark-js

## Correctness

Yuku passes 55,000+ tests from Test262, Babel, TypeScript, and others with full AST matching. Zero failures, zero AST mismatches for JavaScript and JSX.

## Documentation

- [Introduction](https://yuku.dev/): overview, benchmarks, status
- [Parser](https://yuku.dev/parser/): install, quick start, options, diagnostics, traversal, semantic analysis
- [AST reference](https://yuku.dev/parser/ast/): memory model, core types, full node catalog
- [Traverse](https://yuku.dev/parser/traverse/): basic, scoped, semantic, transform traversers

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/yuku-toolchain/yuku
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/yuku-parser
- Zig: `zig fetch --save git+https://github.com/yuku-toolchain/yuku.git`
